title: Bank Official Warns Stock Markets Might Fall
summary: A top official from the Bank of England, Sarah Breeden, said that stock markets around the world are too high and might fall soon. Stock markets are places where people buy and sell shares of companies. When share prices are very high, it means people are paying a lot for them. But there are many problems in the world, like high energy costs and risky investments in new technology like AI. Breeden worries that if many of these problems happen at the same time, stock prices could drop. A big drop could make people feel poorer and reduce spending, and businesses might find it harder to get money. She also warned about 'shadow banking', which is like banks but not as strictly regulated. This system has grown a lot and hasn't been tested in a crisis. The US stock market has been at record highs, but the UK market is also near its highest level.

background_read: Stock markets allow people to invest in companies. When many people buy shares, prices go up. But if there are problems, prices can go down quickly.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sarah Breeden, a deputy governor at the Bank of England
WHAT: Warned that stock markets are too high and might fall
WHERE: In an interview with the BBC
WHY: Because there are many risks like high energy costs, risky AI investments, and unregulated shadow banking

perspectives.description: Thinks markets are too high and need to adjust
